verses.latin_vulgate: Vespere autem sabbati, quæ lucescit in prima sabbati, venit Maria Magdalene, et altera Maria, videre sepulchrum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀψὲ δὲ σαββάτων τῇ ἐπιφωσκούσῃ εἰς μίαν σαββάτων ἦλθεν μαρία ἡ μαγδαληνὴ καὶ ἡ ἄλλη μαριὰ θεωρῆσαι τὸν τάφον
verses.king_james: In the end of the sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first *day* of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce terræmotus factus est magnus. Angelus enim Domini descendit de cælo: et accedens revolvit lapidem, et sedebat super eum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού σεισμὸς ἐγένετο μέγας ἄγγελος γὰρ κυρίου καταβὰς ἐξ οὐρανοῦ προσελθὼν ἀπεκύλισεν τὸν λίθον ἀπὸ τῆς θύρας καὶ ἐκάθητο ἐπάνω αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And, behold, there was a great earthquake: for the angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door, and sat upon it.

verses.latin_vulgate: erat autem aspectus ejus sicut fulgur: et vestimentum ejus sicut nix.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ἡ ἰδέα αὐτοῦ ὡς ἀστραπὴ καὶ τὸ ἔνδυμα αὐτοῦ λευκὸν ὡσεὶ χιών
verses.king_james: His countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snow:

verses.latin_vulgate: Præ timore autem ejus exterriti sunt custodes, et facti sunt velut mortui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπὸ δὲ τοῦ φόβου αὐτοῦ ἐσείσθησαν οἱ τηροῦντες καὶ ἐγένοντο ὡσεὶ νεκροί
verses.king_james: And for fear of him the keepers did shake, and became as dead *men*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem angelus dixit mulieribus: Nolite timere vos: scio enim, quod Jesum, qui crucifixus est, quæritis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ ἄγγελος εἰπεν ταῖς γυναιξίν μὴ φοβεῖσθε ὑμεῖς οἶδα γὰρ ὅτι ἰησοῦν τὸν ἐσταυρωμένον ζητεῖτε
verses.king_james: And the angel answered and said unto the women, Fear not ye: for I know that ye seek Jesus, which was crucified.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est hic: surrexit enim, sicut dixit: venite, et videte locum ubi positus erat Dominus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἔστιν ὧδε ἠγέρθη γὰρ καθὼς εἶπεν δεῦτε ἴδετε τὸν τόπον ὅπου ἔκειτο ὁ κύριος
verses.king_james: He is not here: for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cito euntes, dicite discipulis ejus quia surrexit: et ecce præcedit vos in Galilæam: ibi eum videbitis: ecce prædixi vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ταχὺ πορευθεῖσαι εἴπατε τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ ὅτι ἠγέρθη ἀπὸ τῶν νεκρῶν καὶ ἰδού προάγει ὑμᾶς εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν ἐκεῖ αὐτὸν ὄψεσθε ἰδού εἶπον ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and, behold, he goeth before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him: lo, I have told you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exierunt cito de monumento cum timore et gaudio magno, currentes nuntiare discipulis ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξελθοῦσαι ταχὺ ἀπὸ τοῦ μνημείου μετὰ φόβου καὶ χαρᾶς μεγάλης ἔδραμον ἀπαγγεῖλαι τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy; and did run to bring his disciples word.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce Jesus occurrit illis, dicens: Avete. Illæ autem accesserunt, et tenuerunt pedes ejus, et adoraverunt eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ ἐπορεύοντο ἀπαγγεῖλαι τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ καὶ ἰδού ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀπήντησεν αὐταῖς λέγων χαίρετε αἱ δὲ προσελθοῦσαι ἐκράτησαν αὐτοῦ τοὺς πόδας καὶ προσεκύνησαν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, All hail. And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc ait illis Jesus: Nolite timere: ite, nuntiare fratribus meis ut eant in Galilæam; ibi me videbunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε λέγει αὐταῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς μὴ φοβεῖσθε ὑπάγετε ἀπαγγείλατε τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς μου ἵνα ἀπέλθωσιν εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν κἀκεῖ με ὄψονται
verses.king_james: Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ cum abiissent, ecce quidam de custodibus venerunt in civitatem, et nuntiaverunt principibus sacerdotum omnia quæ facta fuerant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πορευομένων δὲ αὐτῶν ἰδού τινες τῆς κουστωδίας ἐλθόντες εἰς τὴν πόλιν ἀπήγγειλαν τοῖς ἀρχιερεῦσιν ἅπαντα τὰ γενόμενα
verses.king_james: Now when they were going, behold, some of the watch came into the city, and shewed unto the chief priests all the things that were done.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et congregati cum senioribus consilio accepto, pecuniam copiosam dederunt militibus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ συναχθέντες μετὰ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων συμβούλιόν τε λαβόντες ἀργύρια ἱκανὰ ἔδωκαν τοῖς στρατιώταις
verses.king_james: And when they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave large money unto the soldiers,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes: Dicite quia discipuli ejus nocte venerunt, et furati sunt eum, nobis dormientibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγοντες εἴπατε ὅτι οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ νυκτὸς ἐλθόντες ἔκλεψαν αὐτὸν ἡμῶν κοιμωμένων
verses.king_james: Saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole him *away* while we slept.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si hoc auditum fuerit a præside, nos suadebimus ei, et securos vos faciemus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐὰν ἀκουσθῇ τοῦτο ἐπὶ τοῦ ἡγεμόνος ἡμεῖς πείσομεν αὐτὸν καὶ ὑμᾶς ἀμερίμνους ποιήσομεν
verses.king_james: And if this come to the governor's ears, we will persuade him, and secure you.

verses.latin_vulgate: At illi, accepta pecunia, fecerunt sicut erant edocti. Et divulgatum est verbum istud apud Judæos, usque in hodiernum diem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ λαβόντες τὰ ἀργύρια ἐποίησαν ὡς ἐδιδάχθησαν καὶ διεφημίσθη ὁ λόγος οὗτος παρὰ ἰουδαίοις μέχρι τῆς σήμερον
verses.king_james: So they took the money, and did as they were taught: and this saying is commonly reported among the Jews until this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Undecim autem discipuli abierunt in Galilæam in montem ubi constituerat illis Jesus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἕνδεκα μαθηταὶ ἐπορεύθησαν εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν εἰς τὸ ὄρος οὗ ἐτάξατο αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς
verses.king_james: Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountain where Jesus had appointed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et videntes eum adoraverunt: quidam autem dubitaverunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδόντες αὐτὸν προσεκύνησαν αὐτῷ οἱ δὲ ἐδίστασαν
verses.king_james: And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accedens Jesus locutus est eis, dicens: Data est mihi omnis potestas in cælo et in terra:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσελθὼν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς λέγων ἐδόθη μοι πᾶσα ἐξουσία ἐν οὐρανῷ καὶ ἐπὶ γῆς
verses.king_james: And Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: euntes ergo docete omnes gentes: baptizantes eos in nomine Patris, et Filii, et Spiritus Sancti:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πορευθέντες οὖν μαθητεύσατε πάντα τὰ ἔθνη βαπτίζοντες αὐτοὺς εἰς τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ πατρὸς καὶ τοῦ υἱοῦ καὶ τοῦ ἁγίου πνεύματος
verses.king_james: Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:

verses.latin_vulgate: docentes eos servare omnia quæcumque mandavi vobis: et ecce ego vobiscum sum omnibus diebus, usque ad consummationem sæculi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διδάσκοντες αὐτοὺς τηρεῖν πάντα ὅσα ἐνετειλάμην ὑμῖν καὶ ἰδού ἐγὼ μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν εἰμι πάσας τὰς ἡμέρας ἕως τῆς συντελείας τοῦ αἰῶνος ἀμήν
verses.king_james: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, *even* unto the end of the world. Amen.
